sentences.text: This chapter specifies the RVB23 profile family. RVB23 is the first major release of the RVB series of RISC-V Application Processor Profile.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles

sentences.text: RVB profiles are intended to be used for customized 64-bit application processors that will run rich OS stacks, but usually as a custom build of standard OS source-code distributions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles

sentences.text: The approach is to provide a large guaranteed set of relatively inexpensive and/or widely beneficial features but allow optionality for more expensive and/or more targeted extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles

sentences.text: Unlike the RVA profiles, it is explicitly a non-goal of RVB profiles to provide a single standard ISA interface supporting a wide variety of binary kernel and binary application software distributions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles

sentences.text: However, individual software ecosystems may build upon RVB profiles to produce a more targeted standard interface for a certain market.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles

sentences.text: Only user-mode (RVB23U64) and supervisor-mode (RVB23S64) profiles are specified in this family.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles

sentences.text: The RVB23U64 profile specifies the ISA features available to user-mode execution environments in 64-bit RVB applications processors.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile

sentences.text: RV64I is the mandatory base ISA for RVB23U64 and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: As per the unprivileged architecture specification, the `ECALL` instruction causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The following mandatory extensions in RVB23U64 were also mandatory in RVA22U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:m[] Integer multiplication and division. - extlink:a[] Atomic instructions. - Single-precision floating-point instructions. - Double-precision floating-point instructions. - extlink:c[] Compressed instructions. - Bit-manipulation instructions. - extlink:zicsr[] CSR instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: These are implied by presence of F. - Base counters and timers. - Hardware performance counters. - Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support instruction fetch, and any instruction fetches of naturally aligned power-of-2 sizes up to min(ILEN,XLEN) (i.e., 32 bits for RVB23) are atomic. - Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support RsrvEventual. - Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support all atomics in the Zaamo extension. - Misaligned loads and stores to main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must be supported. - Reservation sets are contiguous, naturally aligned, and a maximum of 64 bytes. - Pause hint. - Cache blocks must be 64 bytes in size, naturally aligned in the address space. - Cache-block management instructions. - Cache-block prefetch instructions. - Cache-block zero instructions. - Data-independent execution latency.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The following mandatory extensions are also present in RVA23U64:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Non-temporal locality hints. - Integer conditional operations. - ext:zimop[] May-be-operations. - ext:zcmop[] Compressed may-be-operations. - ext:zcb[] Additional compressed instructions. - Additional floating-point instructions. - Wait-on-reservation-set instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: RVB23U64 has 18 profile options listed below.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: The following extensions are localized options in both RVA23U64 and RVB23U64:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: Vector crypto NIST Algorithms with GCM. - Vector crypto ShangMi Algorithms with GCM.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: The following extensions options are localized options in RVB23U64 but are not present in RVA23U64:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: Vector GCM/GMAC instructions. - Vector crypto NIST algorithms with carryless multiply. - Vector crypto ShangMi algorithms with carryless multiply.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: NOTE: RVA profiles mandate the higher-performing but more expensive GHASH options when adding vector crypto.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: To reduce implementation cost, RVB profiles also allow these carryless multiply options (Zvknc and Zvksc) to implement GCM efficiently, with GHASH available as a separate option.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: Scalar crypto NIST algorithms. - Scalar crypto ShangMi algorithms.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: NOTE: RVA23 profiles drop support for scalar crypto as an option, as the vector extension is now mandatory in RVA23. RVB23 profiles support scalar crypto, as the vector extension is optional in RVB23.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: The following are new development options intended to become mandatory in a later RVB profile:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Development Options

sentences.text: Byte and halfword atomic memory operations. - Compare-and-Swap instructions. - Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must provide `AMOCASQ` level PMA support. - Misaligned loads, stores, and AMOs to main memory regions that do not cross a naturally aligned 16-byte boundary are atomic.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Development Options

sentences.text: The following are expansion options in RVB23U64, but are mandatory in RVA23U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: ext:zfhmin[] Half-precision floating-point.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: NOTE: Unclear if other Zve* extensions should also be supported in RVB.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Vector minimal half-precision floating-point. - Vector basic bit-manipulation instructions. - Vector data-independent execution latency. - Pointer masking, with the execution environment providing a means to select PMLEN=0 and PMLEN=7 at minimum.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: The following extensions are expansion options in both RVA23U64 and RVB23U64:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Scalar half-precision floating-point. - Scalar carryless multiplication. - Landing Pads. - Shadow Stack. - Vector half-precision floating-point. - Scalar BF16 converts. - Vector BF16 converts. - Vector BF16 widening mul-add.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: The following are expansion options for RVB23U64 as they are not intended to be made mandatory in future RVB profiles, but are listed as RVA23U64 development options as they are intended to become mandatory in future RVA profiles.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Vector carryless multiplication.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: There are no transitory options in RVB23U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Optional Extensions > Transitory Options

sentences.text: Implementations are strongly recommended to raise illegal-instruction exceptions on attempts to execute unimplemented opcodes.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23U64 Profile > RVB23U64 Recommendations

sentences.text: The RVB23S64 profile specifies the ISA features available to a supervisor-mode execution environment in 64-bit applications processors. RVB23S64 is based on privileged architecture version 1.13.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile

sentences.text: RV64I is the mandatory base ISA for RVB23S64 and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The `ECALL` instruction operates as per the unprivileged architecture specification.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: An `ECALL` in user mode causes a contained trap to supervisor mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: An `ECALL` in supervisor mode causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The following unprivileged extensions are mandatory:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The RVB23S64 mandatory unprivileged extensions include all the mandatory unprivileged extensions in RVB23U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Zifencei is mandated as it is the only standard way to support instruction-cache coherence in RVB23 application processors. A new instruction-cache coherence mechanism is under development (tentatively named Zjid) which might be added as an option in the future.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The following privileged extensions are mandatory, and are also mandatory in RVA23S64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: *Ss1p13* Supervisor architecture version 1.13.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Ss1p13 supersedes Ss1p12.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NAPOT translation contiguity.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Svnapot is very low cost to provide, so is made mandatory even in RVB.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The `satp` mode Bare must be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Page-Based 39-bit Virtual-Memory System.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Page-fault exceptions are raised when a page is accessed when A bit is clear, or written when D bit is clear.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support hardware page-table reads.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: `stvec.MODE` must be capable of holding the value 0 (Direct).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: When `stvec.MODE=Direct`, `stvec.BASE` must be capable of holding any valid four-byte-aligned address.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: `stval` must be written with the faulting virtual address for load, store, and instruction page-fault, access-fault, and misaligned exceptions, and for breakpoint exceptions that are defined to write an address to stval, other than those caused by execution of the `EBREAK` or `C.EBREAK` instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: For virtual-instruction and illegal-instruction exceptions, `stval` must be written with the faulting instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: For any `hpmcounter` that is not read-only zero, the corresponding bit in `scounteren` must be writable.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Fine-grained address-translation cache invalidation.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: supervisor-mode timer interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Count overflow and mode-based filtering.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: `sstatus.UXL` must be capable of holding the value 2 (i.e., UXLEN=64 must be supported).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: RVB23S64 has the same unprivileged options as RVB23U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: The privileged options in RVB23S64 are listed in the following sections.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: There are no privileged localized options in RVB23S64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Localized Options

sentences.text: There are no privileged development options in RVB23S64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Development Options

sentences.text: The following are privileged expansion options in RVB23S64, but are mandatory in RVA23S64:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Pointer masking, with `senvcfg.PMM` supporting at minimum, settings PMLEN=0 and PMLEN=7.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: The augmented hypervisor extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: When the hypervisor extension is implemented, the following are also mandatory:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: If the hypervisor extension is implemented and pointer masking (Ssnpm) is supported then `henvcfg.PMM` must support at minimum, settings PMLEN=0 and PMLEN=7.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: The following are privileged expansion options in RVB23S64 that are also privileged expansion options in RVA23S64:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Page-based 48-bit virtual-memory system.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Page-based 57-bit virtual-memory system.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: No non-conforming extensions are present.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Attempts to execute unimplemented opcodes or access unimplemented CSRs in the standard or reserved encoding spaces raises an illegal instruction exception that results in a contained trap to the supervisor-mode trap handler.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: NOTE: Ssstrict does not prescribe behavior for the custom encoding spaces or CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: NOTE: Ssstrict definition applies to the execution environment claiming to be RVB23S64-compatible.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: That execution environment will take a contained trap to supervisor-mode (however that trap is implemented, including, but not limited to, emulation/delegation in the outer execution environment).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Ssstrict (and all the other RVB23S64 mandates and options) does not apply to any guest VMs run by a hypervisor.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: An RVB23S64 hypervisor can provide guest VMs that are also RVB23S64-compatible but with an expanded set of emulated standard instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: An RVB23S64 hypervisor can also choose to implement guest VMs that are not RVB23S64 compatible (e.g., only RVA20S64).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Transitions from invalid to valid PTEs will be visible in bounded time without an explicit memory-management fence.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Supervisor-mode pointer masking, with the supervisor execution environment providing a means to select PMLEN=0 and PMLEN=7 at minimum.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Optional Extensions > Expansion Options

sentences.text: Implementations are strongly recommended to raise illegal-instruction exceptions when attempting to execute unimplemented opcodes.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVB23 Profiles > RVB23S64 Profile > RVB23S64 Recommendations
